analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-463

POL-463.title: IMPLEMENT: Polaris Worker Router
POL-463.body:
## Objective

Implement a Polaris-native Worker Router roadmap informed by 9Router concepts while preserving Polaris-owned orchestration, evidence, validation, and finalization boundaries.

## Context

This IMPLEMENT parent comes from ANALYZE source issue [POL-460](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-460/analyze-polaris-worker-router-informed-by-9router). The analysis found that 9Router is useful as a reference for provider/account/model registry, quota tracking, fallback tiers, request logging, and usage analytics, but should not replace Polaris orchestration or become a required runtime dependency.

Polaris already has provider policy and rotation config, provider-selection telemetry, TerminalCliAdapter pre-dispatch fallback, single-worker lifecycle contracts, ordered child selection, and autoresearch scoring foundations. It does not yet have a first-class Worker Router with worker slots, eligibility scoring, quota/cost/trust/capability policy, or router decision telemetry suitable for future SOL inputs.

## Goal

Deliver a staged implementation of the Worker Router architecture, config model, router decision engine, scheduler integration, adapter fallback handling, and telemetry/scoring hooks.

## Scope

* smartdocs/raw/analysis/*worker-router-analysis.md
* smartdocs/specs/raw/**
* smartdocs/specs/active/**
* src/config/**
* src/loop/**
* src/autoresearch/**
* src/cli/**
* src/mcp/**
* src/runtime/**
* tests covering the changed runtime/config/router behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

* Worker Router architecture is documented with explicit boundaries against replacing Polaris orchestration.
* Worker/router config supports role eligibility, provider capabilities, trust, cost, quota/fallback policy, and concurrency/slot controls.
* Router decision logic is deterministic, tested, and emits explainable decision evidence.
* Runtime integration preserves current single-worker behavior by default and only enables multi-slot scheduling when configured.
* Adapter fallback distinguishes provider/pre-dispatch failure from actual worker execution failure.
* Router telemetry and future SOL/autoresearch scoring inputs are captured without weakening existing evidence gates.
* All child issues complete with validation passing.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/config src/loop src/autoresearch src/cli src/mcp

## Ordering

* Children execute in numeric dependency order. Documentation/design comes first, followed by config/types, router core, scheduler slots, adapter fallback/quota integration, and telemetry/SOL scoring.

## Non-goals

* Do not make 9Router a required dependency.
* Do not replace Polaris decomposition, Linear hierarchy, sealed packets, validation, or finalization.
* Do not enable concurrent worker dispatch by default.
* Do not retry a child on another provider after a worker has actually started and produced a result file.

POL-469.title: Emit router telemetry and SOL scoring inputs
POL-469.body:
## Objective

Capture Worker Router decisions and outcomes as durable telemetry and feed them into the autoresearch/SOL scoring path.

## Context

[POL-460](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-460/analyze-polaris-worker-router-informed-by-9router) asks how worker trust level, cost, task type, capability, historical performance, and future SOL scoring should influence routing. Existing dispatch telemetry and autoresearch scoring can be extended instead of introducing a separate analytics system.

## Goal

Record router decisions, candidate rejection reasons, slot assignment, provider fallback, quota/cost/trust/capability inputs, and post-run outcomes in a form that scoreRun/buildProposals can use.

## Scope

* src/loop/dispatch-state.ts
* src/loop/ledger.ts
* src/loop/parent.ts
* src/loop/dispatch.ts
* src/autoresearch/score.ts
* src/autoresearch/gates.ts
* src/autoresearch/proposal.ts
* src/autoresearch/routing.ts
* src/cli/autoresearch.ts
* src/loop/*telemetry*.test.ts
* src/autoresearch/*.test.ts
* smartdocs/specs/raw/**

## Acceptance Criteria

* Router decision telemetry includes selected worker/provider, candidates considered, rejection reasons, score inputs, and fallback attempts.
* Ledger or telemetry records allow correlating router decisions with child completion, validation status, elapsed time, provider/model, and commit/file evidence.
* Autoresearch scoring can consume router outcomes without treating expected fallback as worker failure.
* Proposal generation can recommend policy/config/docs fixes for recurring router failures.
* Tests cover telemetry schema, scoring inputs, and proposal generation for provider exhaustion, quota exhaustion, trust/capability mismatch, and successful fallback.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop/dispatch-state.ts src/loop/parent.test.ts src/loop/dispatch.test.ts src/autoresearch src/cli/autoresearch.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-467](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-467/add-slot-aware-child-scheduling-and-worker-pool-state) and [POL-468](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-468/integrate-router-fallback-with-execution-adapters-and-quota-signals).

## Non-goals

* Do not auto-apply SOL proposals.
* Do not introduce remote analytics or cloud telemetry.
* Do not expose secrets, prompts, or provider credentials in telemetry.

POL-468.title: Integrate router fallback with execution adapters and quota signals
POL-468.body:
## Objective

Connect Worker Router provider decisions to execution adapters and classify quota/fallback outcomes safely.

## Context

`TerminalCliAdapter` already has limited fallback for providers that fail before worker execution starts. 9Router's strongest reusable concept is quota/error-aware fallback, but Polaris must not double-dispatch a child after real worker execution has begun.

## Goal

Route adapter launch attempts through router decision evidence, classify provider failures, and support configured fallback only for safe pre-dispatch/provider-unavailable cases.

## Scope

* src/loop/adapters/**
* src/loop/dispatch.ts
* src/loop/dispatch-state.ts
* src/loop/orphan-recovery.ts
* src/loop/dispatch.test.ts
* src/loop/provider-sealed-packet.test.ts
* src/loop/orphan-recovery.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* Adapter dispatch receives router-selected provider/candidate metadata and records it in dispatch results.
* Provider launch failures are classified separately from worker failures.
* Quota/rate-limit style failures can be marked as provider-unavailable/quota-exhausted when no worker result file exists.
* Fallback never occurs after the worker writes a sealed result or otherwise clearly starts execution.
* Tests cover command-not-found fallback, quota-exhausted fallback, provider policy noFallback, result-file prevents retry, and error evidence in telemetry.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop/adapters src/loop/dispatch.test.ts src/loop/provider-sealed-packet.test.ts src/loop/orphan-recovery.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-466](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-466/implement-deterministic-worker-router-decision-engine). May run after or alongside [POL-467](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-467/add-slot-aware-child-scheduling-and-worker-pool-state) if shared files are coordinated carefully.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement provider-specific live quota API clients unless a provider already exposes a simple local signal.
* Do not proxy LLM requests or implement an OpenAI-compatible gateway.
* Do not change worker result schema without preserving backward compatibility.

POL-467.title: Add slot-aware child scheduling and worker pool state
POL-467.body:
## Objective

Integrate Worker Router decisions into child scheduling and worker-pool state while preserving current single-worker defaults.

## Context

The parent loop currently selects the next open child and dispatches one worker at a time. [POL-460](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-460/analyze-polaris-worker-router-informed-by-9router) specifically asks how Polaris should maintain a worker pool, such as three active non-Claude workers, and assign the next unblocked child when a worker finishes.

## Goal

Add slot-aware scheduling primitives and state transitions that can claim multiple eligible child/provider slots when configured, without changing default one-child/session behavior.

## Scope

* src/loop/parent.ts
* src/loop/dispatch.ts
* src/loop/lifecycle.ts
* src/loop/checkpoint.ts
* src/loop/run-preflight.ts
* src/cluster-state/**
* src/runtime/scheduling/**
* src/mcp/tools/**
* src/loop/parent.test.ts
* src/loop/lifecycle.test.ts
* src/loop/run-preflight.test.ts
* src/cluster-state/*.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* Default behavior remains `max_concurrent = 1` and existing supervised/auto run semantics continue to pass.
* Configured worker pool limits can express multiple active non-Claude workers without violating role policy.
* Scheduler selects the next unblocked child using cluster dependencies and router eligibility evidence, not chat reasoning.
* Slot claims are durable in cluster/current state and expire or recover safely on orphaned workers.
* Tests cover one-slot compatibility, multi-slot selection, blocked dependency exclusion, claim expiry, and worker-finish-next-child assignment.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop/parent.test.ts src/loop/lifecycle.test.ts src/loop/run-preflight.test.ts src/cluster-state src/runtime/scheduling src/mcp/tools

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-466](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-466/implement-deterministic-worker-router-decision-engine).

## Non-goals

* Do not enable multi-worker scheduling by default.
* Do not loosen one-child packet scope or sealed result requirements.
* Do not bypass cluster `blockedBy` dependencies.

POL-466.title: Implement deterministic Worker Router decision engine
POL-466.body:
## Objective

Implement the pure Worker Router decision engine that selects eligible worker/provider candidates and returns explainable routing evidence.

## Context

Provider selection currently lives in dispatch code and chooses by CLI override, role policy, rotation, or first provider. The Worker Router should make this explicit and testable before runtime integration.

## Goal

Create a deterministic router module that evaluates child/task inputs, role authority, route/domain metadata, provider capabilities, trust/cost/quota policy, fallback state, and slot availability.

## Scope

* src/loop/router/**
* src/loop/dispatch.ts
* src/loop/dispatch.test.ts
* src/loop/adapters/types.ts
* src/loop/worker-packet.ts
* src/loop/worker-packet.test.ts
* src/config/**

## Acceptance Criteria

* Router exposes typed inputs/outputs for candidate eligibility, rejection reasons, selected provider/worker, and scoring evidence.
* Decision logic is pure and deterministic for identical inputs.
* Router supports current behavior as compatibility mode when no router config is present.
* Router refuses forbidden providers and reports why: role-disabled, not-in-policy, quota-exhausted, trust-too-low, capability-mismatch, cost-policy, or no-slot.
* Unit tests cover happy paths, tie-breaking, rejection reasons, fallback ordering, and compatibility mode.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop/router src/loop/dispatch.test.ts src/loop/worker-packet.test.ts src/config

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-465](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-465/add-worker-router-config-and-policy-types).

## Non-goals

* Do not change parent-loop concurrency behavior in this child.
* Do not launch workers from the router module.
* Do not read or write runtime state from the pure router decision function.

POL-465.title: Add Worker Router config and policy types
POL-465.body:
## Objective

Add typed configuration and validation for the Worker Router policy surface.

## Context

The existing config has `execution.providers`, `execution.rotation`, `allowCrossAgentFallback`, and `providerPolicy`. The Worker Router needs a first-class, backward-compatible policy model for eligibility, capabilities, trust, cost, quota, fallback, and slot controls.

## Goal

Define config schema/types/defaults/validation for router policy while preserving current configs and default single-worker behavior.

## Scope

* src/config/schema.ts
* src/config/schema.json
* src/config/defaults.ts
* src/config/validator.ts
* src/config/validator.test.ts
* src/config/loader.test.ts
* src/cli/init.ts
* src/cli/init.test.ts
* polaris.config.json documentation references only if needed

## Acceptance Criteria

* Config supports worker/provider registry metadata: role eligibility, capabilities/task types, trust tier, cost tier, quota policy, fallback eligibility, max active slots, and default worker pool limits.
* Defaults preserve existing runtime behavior: no concurrent worker expansion and no new automatic cross-provider delegation unless configured.
* Validator rejects unknown providers, invalid slot counts, invalid trust/cost/capability values, and ambiguous fallback policy.
* Tests cover backward compatibility and invalid config cases.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/config src/cli/init.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-464](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-464/document-worker-router-architecture-and-invariants).

## Non-goals

* Do not integrate router decisions into dispatch in this child.
* Do not change runtime provider selection behavior yet.
* Do not require live provider quota APIs.

POL-464.title: Document Worker Router architecture and invariants
POL-464.body:
## Objective

Write the Worker Router architecture spec and record the 9Router-informed design boundaries before runtime code changes.

## Context

[POL-460](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-460/analyze-polaris-worker-router-informed-by-9router) asks Polaris to learn from 9Router without replacing Polaris orchestration. This child turns the analysis into repo-local architecture guidance for subsequent implementation children.

## Goal

Produce a Worker Router spec that defines responsibilities, non-responsibilities, data flow, router decision evidence, scheduler boundaries, and migration stages.

## Scope

* smartdocs/raw/analysis/*worker-router-analysis.md
* smartdocs/specs/raw/**
* smartdocs/specs/active/**
* smartdocs/architecture/**
* src/loop/POLARIS.md
* src/loop/adapters/POLARIS.md
* src/config/POLARIS.md

## Acceptance Criteria

* Spec compares 9Router concepts against Polaris-owned orchestration boundaries.
* Spec defines worker/provider registry, slot pool, eligibility, trust, cost, quota, fallback, route/domain, and SOL telemetry concepts.
* Spec states default behavior remains single-worker and no 9Router dependency is introduced.
* Route docs are updated only where the spec materially changes operational guidance.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop src/config

## Ordering

* None. This child must run before config/types and runtime integration children.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement runtime router code.
* Do not promote draft docs to doctrine without the existing SmartDocs promotion workflow.
* Do not make 9Router a required dependency.

POL-460.title: ANALYZE: Polaris Worker Router informed by 9Router
